name: Credit Union of Denver
description_short: Credit Union of Denver is a member-owned financial cooperative offering checking, savings, loans, and credit products with a 95-year history serving the Denver area.
description_long: Credit Union of Denver has been operating for 95 years as a member-owned credit union, establishing itself as a community-focused financial institution in the Denver market. The organization provides a full spectrum of consumer banking services including deposit accounts, lending products, and digital banking solutions. Their product lineup encompasses savings accounts (including money market and certificate of deposit options), checking accounts with rewards, auto loans, personal loans, home equity lines of credit, second mortgages, credit cards, and skip-a-pay payment assistance. The credit union distinguishes itself through competitive rate offerings, community involvement (evidenced by charitable initiatives like pet calendar donations and children's hospital blanket drives), and a strong emphasis on digital banking capabilities including mobile deposit, biometric login, Zelle integration, and financial wellness tools. Their routing number 307075259 and extensive branch and ATM network provide accessibility to members. However, as a credit union, membership eligibility may be required, and their service area appears to be primarily Colorado-based, limiting convenience for non-local members despite digital banking options. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Competitive rates on savings products (5.00% APY on Interest Rewards Checking up to $10,000; 3.65% APY on 6-month certificates)
Low auto loan rates starting at 4.95% APR for both new and used vehicles
No-fee balance transfer feature on MasterCard with rates as low as 10.99% VAPR
Comprehensive mobile banking with biometric login, mobile deposit, and Zelle integration
Free credit score and report access through digital banking platform
Financial wellness tools including savings goal tracking and financial planning features
Skip-a-pay payment assistance program exclusively available to digital banking members
cons: Membership eligibility requirements typical of credit unions may restrict access compared to banks
Service area appears limited to Colorado region based on Denver-centric marketing and branch/ATM locator functionality
Limited information on website about membership eligibility criteria or account opening requirements
HELOC rates starting at 6.25% VAPR are higher than personal loan rates, potentially limiting home equity product appeal
No mention of business banking or commercial services despite offering personal and consumer products
best_for: Denver area residents seeking competitive savings and loan rates with strong digital banking capabilities
Auto loan shoppers comparing rates in the Colorado region
Credit union members prioritizing community involvement and charitable initiatives
Digital banking users wanting advanced mobile features like biometric login and mobile deposit
diagnosis: Credit Union of Denver is best for Colorado-based consumers seeking competitive deposit and loan rates with strong digital banking infrastructure, particularly those valuing community-focused financial institutions. The main caveat is that membership may have eligibility restrictions and the credit union's primary service area appears limited to Colorado, making it less convenient for members outside the region despite robust online banking options.
services: Checking accounts with rewards (Interest Rewards Checking up to 5.00% APY)
Savings accounts (Money Market accounts, Youth Savings accounts)
Certificates of deposit (6-month terms at 3.65% APY; IRA certificates)
Auto loans (new and used vehicles at 4.95% APR)
Home equity lines of credit (80% LTV at 6.25% VAPR starting)
MasterCard credit card (Platinum with no-fee balance transfers)
Mobile banking (deposit, transfers, Zelle, biometric login)
Financial wellness tools (credit score access, financial planning, account snapshots)
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
